definition: A strong, impulsive price move composed of full-bodied candles that breaks through a prior swing high/low or range boundary with momentum, creating a Fair Value Gap and closing beyond the level. It confirms a Change in the State of Delivery or market structure shift, distinguishing genuine breakouts from consolidation. Typically follows a liquidity sweep or engagement with a higher-timeframe point of interest.
identification_rules: Price moves beyond a prior swing high (bullish) or swing low (bearish) with a strong, momentum-driven candle or series of candles; the close must be decisively beyond the level, not merely a wick.
The displacement candle(s) are full-bodied (large real body, small wicks) and cover a large price range relative to recent candles.
A Fair Value Gap (three-candle imbalance) is left behind in the wake of the move, indicating one-sided, inefficient price delivery.
Displacement often follows a liquidity sweep, stop raid, or engagement with a higher-timeframe point of interest (e.g., PD Array).
If price exits the range boundary but promptly returns inside without sustained follow-through, displacement is absent — the move is classified as a deviation or liquidity sweep.
Displacement is confirmed only when price continues to expand directionally beyond the broken level rather than oscillating back toward equilibrium of the prior range.

Distinguish true displacement from a fake Change in the State of Delivery by confirming that a higher time frame PD Array was reached prior to the move; absence of HTF engagement suggests the move may be a false signal.
Displacement can be identified on multiple timeframes; higher timeframe displacement has stronger trend continuation significance.
Displacement may be outward (breaking out of a consolidation range) or inward (returning into a range), with the latter often signaling a structural shift.
After displacement, the range from start to end can be used with Fibonacci; the 0.5 level divides discount and premium zones.
trading_implication: Displacement confirms directional bias and validates a market structure shift or change in state of delivery. It is not an entry signal itself; traders wait for a retracement into the resulting Fair Value Gap, Order Block, or OTE zone (discount for longs, premium for shorts) before entering. Stops are placed beyond the displacement origin or protected swing. Targets are opposing liquidity (swing highs/lows, previous day high/low) for a minimum 2R reward. Absence of displacement warns of consolidation or false breakouts, prompting a mean-reversion or stand-aside bias.
common_mistakes: Treating any move outside a range boundary as displacement — a move that immediately reverts back inside the range is a liquidity sweep, not displacement.
Confusing a wick-only sweep with displacement; displacement requires a full-bodied close beyond the level, not just a probe.
Treating a slow, overlapping move as displacement when no Fair Value Gap or aggressive full-bodied closure is present.
Entering a reversal or continuation trade before displacement confirms the structural break.
Chasing the displacement candle directly without waiting for a retracement entry.
Expecting displacement to occur without a preceding liquidity sweep or reaction at a key point of interest.
Attempting to trade directional breakouts during Seek and Destroy conditions where neither side of the range produces displacement.
Treating a Change in the State of Delivery as valid displacement without confirming that price first engaged a higher time frame PD Array.
Misidentifying a displacement during London when price has not reached a higher time frame level.
Confusing displacement with market structure shift (MSS) — they are related but distinct.
Failing to distinguish displacement from simple price retracement or consolidation.
Trading displacement on low timeframe without considering higher timeframe trend direction.
Giving equal weight to all displacements regardless of strength and quality.

related_concepts.term: Change in the State of Delivery

merge_notes: All 6 input cards are from ICT school (5 explicitly ICT, 1 Price Action but with ICT concepts). No school conflict. The 'Expansion' cards were merged as they describe the same concept as Displacement (aggressive directional move). Nuance preserved: displacement requires full-bodied candles, FVG, and close beyond level; special case of Seek and Destroy conditions noted; multiple timeframe alignment emphasized.
